first_principles_validity_hypothesis: The prediction "So one of the things that we proposed in this paper is so..." is forced by the underlying constraint "Fundamentally the reason for that is that software is cha..."; if the constraint is not binding the prediction may still hold by accident but the binding case is the load-bearing version of this candidate.
why_novel_vs_speaker: The speaker introduces the prediction (snippet S031) and the first-principle (snippet S001) in separate contexts; the speaker does not state that the second forces the first.
why_potentially_useful: A prediction that is forced by a first-principle is more falsifiable than a free-floating prediction: if the principle holds and the prediction fails, one of them is wrong, which is a binary research question.
